title: Te Arotakenga Hōtoke Taiao / The Winter Impact Assessment
subject: Technology
culturalContext: Students will investigate how winter conditions affect the Mangakōtukutuku Stream (Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku), a significant waterway flowing through Ngāti Kahungunu rohe. They will explore traditional Māori knowledge of seasonal changes (maramataka) and modern environmental monitoring to understand the stream's health during takurua (winter), connecting to local stories about the stream as a life-giver and source of mahinga kai.
learningObjectives: Design and implement a water quality testing protocol for the Mangakōtukutuku Stream using digital sensors and traditional observation methods
Analyze winter water quality data to identify impacts of seasonal changes and potential human activities on the stream ecosystem
Create a digital presentation that integrates scientific data with mātauranga Māori perspectives on winter environmental management
Propose a technology-based solution to address one identified environmental impact on the stream

activities.title: Whakawhanaungatanga ki Te Awa
activities.description: Students visit Mangakōtukutuku Stream with a local kaumātua who shares pūrākau (traditional stories) about the stream and demonstrates traditional methods of assessing water health through observation of flora, fauna, and water clarity

activities.title: He Tātari Wai mō Takurua
activities.description: Students use water testing kits, digital pH meters, turbidity tubes, and temperature sensors to collect winter baseline data at three points along the stream, documenting their methods with digital devices

activities.title: Te Hōhonutanga o te Raraunga
activities.description: Working in pairs, students analyze their collected data using spreadsheet software, creating graphs and charts to visualize patterns and correlations between different water quality parameters

activities.title: Te Ao Hurihuri
activities.description: Students research modern winter environmental challenges affecting the stream (e.g., stormwater runoff, changing rainfall patterns) and traditional Māori winter practices for protecting waterways

activities.title: He Rautaki Whakamauri Awa
activities.description: Using design thinking principles, students develop a technological solution (e.g., simple filtration system, community alert app, educational resource) to address one identified impact on the stream's winter health

resources: Local kaumātua from Ngāti Kahungunu with knowledge of Mangakōtukutuku Stream
LaMotte Water Monitoring Kits or similar water testing equipment
NIWA's Freshwater Ecosystem Health educational resources
Te Maramataka - Māori Lunar Calendar resources from local marae

tasks: Water quality data collection and analysis report
Digital presentation integrating scientific and cultural perspectives
Prototype or design proposal for technological solution

nzcAlignment: Technological Practice: Planning for practice, brief development
Technological Knowledge: Technological modelling, products and systems
Nature of Technology: Characteristics of technology, characteristics of technological outcomes
Science: Living World - Ecology, Planet Earth and Beyond - Earth systems
